explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Third-party articles reference legislative acts that require the evaluation and fixing of biased algorithms.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party article, "How biased algorithms perpetuate inequality," provides evidence for **external_accountability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. It supports **fairness** by discussing types of algorithmic bias and proposing steps to avoid it, and highlights the negative consequences of biased algorithms. The article also contributes to **transparency** by describing how AI systems function and mentioning AI use cases for decision-making. Furthermore, it touches upon **governance** and **external_accountability** by referencing legislative acts requiring evaluation and fixing of biased algorithms.
relevant_sources.title: How biased algorithms perpetuate inequality

fairness.findings: Annual reports mention AI risks such as bias. Third-party articles discuss types of algorithmic bias, propose steps to avoid it, and highlight the negative consequences of biased algorithms.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: The Fox Corporation 10-K Annual Report (FY2025) provides evidence for **governance**, **fairness**, and **privacy**. This sec filing supports governance by detailing the Audit Committee's oversight of AI risk policies and practices, and by acknowledging the legal and regulatory landscape for AI, including requirements for disclosures and record retention. Evidence for fairness and privacy is found in mentions of AI risks such as bias and privacy violations, implying a need for policies to mitigate these issues.
relevant_sources.title: Fox Corporation 10-K Annual Report (FY2025)

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party article, "How biased algorithms perpetuate inequality," provides evidence for **external_accountability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. It supports **fairness** by discussing types of algorithmic bias and proposing steps to avoid it, and highlights the negative consequences of biased algorithms. The article also contributes to **transparency** by describing how AI systems function and mentioning AI use cases for decision-making. Furthermore, it touches upon **governance** and **external_accountability** by referencing legislative acts requiring evaluation and fixing of biased algorithms.
relevant_sources.title: How biased algorithms perpetuate inequality

governance.findings: Press releases reference platform and data strategies for AI implementation. Annual reports detail the Audit Committee's oversight of AI risk policies and practices, acknowledging the legal and regulatory landscape for AI, including requirements for disclosures and record retention. Privacy policies describe data sharing with third-party advertising and analytics companies, and mention AI features like chatbots and the potential for monitoring AI chat sessions. Third-party articles also reference legislative acts requiring evaluation and fixing of biased algorithms.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release for FOX's OneFOX converged media platform provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by referencing a platform strategy and data strategy, indicating a structured approach to AI implementation. Transparency is supported through explicit mentions of AI-driven technology, predictive models, and AI-driven audience modeling, which detail the capabilities and mechanisms used for personalized ad experiences.
relevant_sources.title: FOX Launches an AI-Driven Converged Media Platform

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report on Fox News spreading misinformation about AI-generated SNAP videos provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The source highlights the importance of human-centered AI policies, government tracking of AI incidents for risk management, and accountability for AI risks, all of which are key components of robust AI governance.
relevant_sources.title: Fox News Spreads Misinformation After Airing Racist AI-Generated SNAP Videos

relevant_sources.summary: The Fox Corporation 10-K Annual Report (FY2025) provides evidence for **governance**, **fairness**, and **privacy**. This sec filing supports governance by detailing the Audit Committee's oversight of AI risk policies and practices, and by acknowledging the legal and regulatory landscape for AI, including requirements for disclosures and record retention. Evidence for fairness and privacy is found in mentions of AI risks such as bias and privacy violations, implying a need for policies to mitigate these issues.
relevant_sources.title: Fox Corporation 10-K Annual Report (FY2025)

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports the privacy pillar by detailing how personal information, including network activity and inferred interests, is collected and used for targeted advertising and personalization, and by outlining user opt-out rights for data sales and profiling. The governance pillar is supported by the policy's description of data sharing with third-party advertising and analytics companies, implying structured vendor oversight and data processing for business purposes, as well as its mention of AI features like chatbots and the potential for monitoring AI chat sessions.
relevant_sources.title: Fox Corporation Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party article, "How biased algorithms perpetuate inequality," provides evidence for **external_accountability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: Annual reports mention AI risks such as privacy violations. Privacy policies detail how personal information, including network activity and inferred interests, is collected and used for targeted advertising and personalization. These policies also outline user opt-out rights for data sales and profiling, and mention AI features like chatbots and the potential for monitoring AI chat sessions.

transparency.findings: Published materials explicitly mention AI-driven technology, predictive models, and AI-driven audience modeling, detailing their capabilities and mechanisms for personalized ad experiences. These materials also describe the use of AI-powered semantic search for content delivery and its impact on user experience through improved search accuracy and dynamic information, as well as how AI systems function and their use cases for decision-making.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

It supports governance by referencing a platform strategy and data strategy, indicating a structured approach to AI implementation. Transparency is supported through explicit mentions of AI-driven technology, predictive models, and AI-driven audience modeling, which detail the capabilities and mechanisms used for personalized ad experiences.
relevant_sources.title: FOX Launches an AI-Driven Converged Media Platform

relevant_sources.summary: This customer story, "FOX Sports Reimagines the Fan Experience," provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It describes FOX Sports' use of AI-powered semantic search to deliver more context-aware results and enhance fan engagement, implying transparency in how AI is applied for content delivery and its impact on user experience through improved search accuracy and dynamic information.
relevant_sources.title: FOX Sports Reimagines the Fan Experience

The article also contributes to **transparency** by describing how AI systems function and mentioning AI use cases for decision-making. summary.overall_findings: Fox's published materials explicitly mention AI-driven technology, predictive models, and AI-driven audience modeling as part of its transparency disclosures. These disclosures, along with evidence for fairness, privacy, governance, and external accountability, mean 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars have documented evidence. For instance, privacy policies detail how personal information, including network activity and inferred interests, is collected and used for targeted advertising and personalization, while governance press releases reference a platform strategy for AI implementation. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability or oversight, based on a review of 14 publicly available sources.
